name: apply-patch-parity
doc: apply_patch parity beyond scenario 22 (which covers add/delete/multi-file deny). Covers `Move to` (update+rename judged on the NEW path) and a malformed envelope's fail-open. NOTE (audit finding, out of this lot's file ownership): normalize.ts hardcodes `phase: "pre"` for every apply_patch event regardless of the real `hook_event_name`, so a genuine Codex `PostToolUse` apply_patch payload still routes to handlePre today, not handlePost — the new per-file Post fan-out (handle-post.ts + runtime/post-fanout.ts) is unit-tested directly in test/apply-patch-post.test.ts (bypassing handleHook) until that routing bug is fixed by whoever owns normalize.ts.

steps.doc: `*** Update File: old.ts` + `*** Move to: new.ts` with a 150-line hunk. parseApplyPatch renames the entry's path to the Move target (apply-patch.ts:76-78) BEFORE applyPatchGate judges it, so the SOLID deny names the NEW path, not the pre-move one.

One envelope carrying add+update+delete+move, ALL compliant → allow (empty stdout). Proves the fan-out doesn't blanket-deny a mixed-op patch when every hunk is clean, and that delete/move hunks contribute no content to judge.

tool_input.command:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: fresh.ts
+const a = 1;
*** Update File: keep.ts
@@
+const b = 2;
*** Delete File: gone.ts
*** Update File: renamed-old.ts
*** Move to: renamed-new.ts
@@
+const c = 3;
*** End Patch

steps.doc: Malformed apply_patch body (no recognizable `*** Begin/Add/Update/Delete` markers) → parseApplyPatch returns [] → normalize.ts leaves `files` undefined → the event carries no filePath/content/command at all. Fails OPEN: no crash, no deny, exit 0.

tool_input.command: this is not a patch, just prose
